plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet the single-name limit.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for greater income stability.
Buy XLF at 5% to diversify into financials.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer for capital preservation.
These moves reduce overall risk and leverage.
conservative.read: This mode targets capital preservation with a potential ~1.5% monthly return. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, favoring broad index and income. Expect lower volatility and drawdown, but also more modest growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name limit.
Reduce SOXL and SPAL to 3% and 2% respectively, lowering leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase CRWD to 18% to lean into a strong uptrend.
Buy MSFT at 5% for quality tech exposure.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer.
These adjustments balance growth with risk management.
balanced.read: This mode aims for risk-first growth, targeting a ~2.5% monthly return. It balances broad index exposure with quality tech and some reduced leveraged ETF exposure. Expect moderate volatility and drawdown, with decent growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name limit.
Increase SOXL to 8% to leverage semiconductor exposure.
Increase CRWD to 20% to concentrate in a strong uptrend.
Buy TECS at 7% as a high-scoring scanner setup.
Buy XLF at 5% for diversification.
Maintain a 5% cash buffer, accepting higher risk.
aggressive.read: This aggressive mode targets ~4% monthly growth but comes with significantly higher risk. It leans into leveraged ETFs and concentrated positions. Be aware of increased volatility, potential for larger drawdowns, and daily decay/rebalancing effects of leveraged ETFs.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name limit.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 30% for a stronger income focus.
Trim QQQ to 10% to shift towards income.
Maintain a 15% cash buffer.
These moves prioritize steady distributions and lower volatility.
income.read: This mode prioritizes steady income, targeting ~1% monthly distributions. It focuses on income and broad index ETFs, with minimal exposure to high-beta or leveraged assets. While aiming for lower drawdown, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market selloffs.
